OTHER KEVIN (RECEIVER) SINGER Motion For Order Authorizing Disbursement Of Funds is GRANTED as requested. Opposition filed on January 27, 2026 is considered and stricken. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL VS. SANTOS & URRUTIA ASSOCIATES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 28, 2026. Line 2.3. DEFENDANT KEVIN O'CONNOR MOTION TO STRIKE Plaintiff's Untimely Reply re: Motion for Attorney's Fees is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005. To the extent Defendant objects to the untimely filing of the reply in support of the motion for attorney's fees, the objection is overruled. Reply was timely filed and served on January 21, 2026. While that filing was rejected, the City immediately re-filed on January 22, 2025 correcting the hearing department number, Defendant points to no substantive difference between the two filings. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 28, 2026. Line 15. DEFENDANT JANET ROSMARY PAUCAS CAMARGO, JUAN OCHOA DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend within 5 days of notice of entry of order, for Plaintiff to allege the specific facts (1) supporting the creation of an implied tenancy at will (i.e. the facts by which Plaintiff's consent is implied) and (2) supporting Plaintiff's position that the Defendants are not entitled to the specific notice requirements and relocation benefits for residents of HUD-subsidized housing which is being demolished. This matter will be heard in department 505 by the Honorable Michelle Tong at 1:30 p.m. per the order of the presiding judge. =(505/MT) Parties may appear in-person, or via Zoom Video Conference (Meeting ID: 160 4132 4666 Passcode: 872834). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
